---
title: "Smartwaches"
author: "Isaac"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```


```{r cars}
deidentified_data <- read.csv("deidentified_data.csv")

```



```{r}
# 1. Cargar la librería
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)

# 2. Eliminar filas donde ECG es nulo (es nuestro gold standard)

dataset_limpio <- deidentified_data %>%
  drop_na(ECG)

#filas antes vs filas después
nrow(deidentified_data)
nrow(dataset_limpio)
```

```{r}
# ==========================================
# 1. LOAD DATA & INITIAL AUDIT
# ==========================================
library(tidyverse)
library(viridis)

raw_data <- read.csv("deidentified_data.csv", na.strings = c("NA", "NaN", "", " "))

# Standardizing sensor names (handling dots/spaces)
actual_sensors <- c("ECG", "Apple.Watch", "Empatica", "Garmin", "Fitbit", "Miband", "Biovotion")
actual_sensors <- actual_sensors[actual_sensors %in% names(raw_data)]

# ==========================================
# 2. DATA QUALITY AUDIT (PRE-FILTERING)
# ==========================================
# Calculate how "empty" each row is
void_analysis <- raw_data %>%
  mutate(empty_count = rowSums(is.na(select(., all_of(actual_sensors)))),
         is_totally_void = empty_count == length(actual_sensors))

# ==========================================
# 3. GRAPH: VOID DISTRIBUTION (THE "SILENT" ROWS)
# ==========================================
p_voids <- void_analysis %>%
  group_by(Activity) %>%
  summarise(Total_Rows = n(), 
            Void_Rows = sum(is_totally_void)) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = -Activity) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = Activity, y = value, fill = name)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "dodge", color = "black", alpha = 0.8) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Total_Rows" = "#34495e", "Void_Rows" = "#e74c3c"),
                    labels = c("Total Recorded", "Totally Empty")) +
  labs(title = "Data Presence Audit", subtitle = "Red bars show time segments with zero sensor activity",
       x = "Activity Phase", y = "Number of Seconds (Rows)") +
  theme_minimal()

# ==========================================
# 4. MATERIALISING MINABLE VIEW
# ==========================================
minable_view <- raw_data %>%
  filter(!is.na(ECG)) %>%
  filter(if_any(all_of(actual_sensors), ~ !is.na(.))) %>%
  filter(ECG >= 30 & ECG <= 220)

# ==========================================
# 5. GRAPH: SENSOR DENSITY MAP (DEEP BLUE SCALE)
# ==========================================
# We calculate "Data Presence" (100 - Missing %)
# We use a square root scale to amplify differences in sparse data
p_density_map <- minable_view %>%
  group_by(ID) %>%
  summarise(across(all_of(actual_sensors), ~mean(!is.na(.)) * 100)) %>%
  pivot_longer(-ID, names_to = "Sensor", values_to = "Presence") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = Sensor, y = as.factor(ID), fill = Presence)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", size = 0.1) +
  # Deep Blue Scale: Light blue = almost empty, Dark Navy = Full data
  scale_fill_distiller(palette = "Blues", direction = 1, name = "% Active") +
  labs(title = "High-Resolution Data Density Map", 
       subtitle = "Darker blue indicates more continuous signal per participant",
       x = "Sensor / Device", y = "Participant ID") +
  theme_minimal() + 
  theme(axis.text.y = element_text(size = 5),
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

# ==========================================
# 6. GRAPH: BPM ANALYSIS (INCLUDING BIOVOTION)
# ==========================================
p_activity_bpm <- minable_view %>%
  select(Activity, all_of(actual_sensors)) %>%
  pivot_longer(-Activity, names_to = "Device", values_to = "BPM") %>%
  filter(!is.na(BPM)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = Device, y = BPM, fill = Device)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.color = "#c0392b", outlier.alpha = 0.3, width = 0.6) +
  facet_wrap(~Activity) +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Paired") +
  labs(title = "Heart Rate Signal Distribution", 
       subtitle = "Comparison of Biovotion and wrist-worn devices against reference",
       x = "", y = "Beats Per Minute (BPM)") +
  theme_bw() + 
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1), legend.position = "none")

# ==========================================
# 7. EXECUTION
# ==========================================
print(p_voids)
print(p_density_map)
print(p_activity_bpm)
```

```{r}
# ==========================================
# EXTRA: ORDERED ACTIVITY DATA COUNT
# ==========================================
library(tidyverse)

# 1. Identify sensors
target_sensors <- c("ECG", "Apple.Watch", "Empatica", "Garmin", "Fitbit", "Miband", "Biovotion")
available_sensors <- names(raw_data)[names(raw_data) %in% target_sensors]

# 2. Calculate non-null counts per Activity and Sensor
activity_counts <- raw_data %>%
  group_by(Activity) %>%
  summarise(across(all_of(available_sensors), ~ sum(!is.na(.)))) %>%
  pivot_longer(-Activity, names_to = "Sensor", values_to = "Valid_Records") %>%
  # Filter out combinations with 0 data to keep it clean
  filter(Valid_Records > 0)

# 3. Plot: Ordered Bars by Data Volume
p_activity_bars <- ggplot(activity_counts, 
                          aes(x = reorder(Sensor, -Valid_Records), 
                              y = Valid_Records, 
                              fill = Activity)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge", color = "white", alpha = 0.9) +
  # Professional Blue Palette
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Blues") +
  labs(title = "Data Volume by Sensor and Activity",
       subtitle = "Sorted by total valid heart rate records (Non-Null values)",
       x = "Recording Device", 
       y = "Total Valid Seconds (BPM)") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
        panel.grid.major.x = element_blank())

# 4. Display
print(p_activity_bars)
```
```{r}
# ==========================================
# TOTAL RECOVERY: AUTO-DETECT ACTIVITIES
# ==========================================
library(tidyverse)

# 1. Carga limpia y detección de columnas
raw_data <- read.csv("deidentified_data.csv", na.strings = c("NA", "NaN", "", " "))

# Limpiamos nombres de sensores (Biovotion incluido)
sensors <- c("ECG", "Apple.Watch", "Empatica", "Garmin", "Fitbit", "Miband", "Biovotion")
available <- names(raw_data)[names(raw_data) %in% sensors]

# 2. Limpieza radical de la columna Activity
# Esto elimina espacios, saltos de línea y basura de texto
raw_data$Activity <- trimws(as.character(raw_data$Activity))

# 3. Listar actividades reales (Esto nos dirá qué hay exactamente)
lista_actividades <- unique(raw_data$Activity)
lista_actividades <- lista_actividades[!is.na(lista_actividades)]

# 4. CREAR Y MOSTRAR TODAS LAS TABLAS POSIBLES
# Este bucle va a crear una tabla por cada actividad que exista en tu archivo
for (act in lista_actividades) {
  
  cat("\n==========================================\n")
  cat("TABLE FOR ACTIVITY:", act, "\n")
  cat("==========================================\n")
  
  resumen <- raw_data %>%
    filter(Activity == act) %>%
    group_by(Skin.Tone) %>%
    summarise(across(all_of(available), ~ round(mean(., na.rm = TRUE), 1)), .groups = "drop") %>%
    # Aseguramos los 6 tonos de piel
    complete(Skin.Tone = 1:6) %>%
    # Si sale NaN, ponemos un guion "-"
    mutate(across(-Skin.Tone, ~ ifelse(is.nan(.), "-", as.character(.))))
  
  print(resumen, row.names = FALSE)
}
```
```{r}
# ==========================================
# PATTERN ANALYSIS: ACTIVITY SEQUENCE
# ==========================================
library(tidyverse)

# 1. Detectar columna de tiempo automáticamente
detect_time <- ifelse("Time" %in% names(raw_data), "Time", names(raw_data)[1])

# 2. Analizar el orden cronológico de las actividades por paciente
sequence_report <- raw_data %>%
  group_by(ID, Activity) %>%
  summarise(
    Start_Second = min(get(detect_time), na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(ID, Start_Second) %>%
  group_by(ID) %>%
  summarise(
    Full_Sequence = paste(Activity, collapse = " -> "),
    Steps = n()
  )

# 3. Ver cuáles son los patrones más comunes
patterns_summary <- sequence_report %>%
  group_by(Full_Sequence) %>%
  tally(sort = TRUE)

# ==========================================
# OUTPUT
# ==========================================
print("--- MOST COMMON ACTIVITY SEQUENCES ---")
print(patterns_summary)

# Ejemplo visual para el reporte:
# Ver cómo saltan las actividades en el tiempo para el primer ID
sample_id <- unique(raw_data$ID)[1]
p_seq_viz <- raw_data %>%
  filter(ID == sample_id) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = get(detect_time), y = Activity, color = Activity)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5) +
  labs(title = paste("Timeline for Subject:", sample_id),
       x = "Time (Seconds)", y = "Activity Name") +
  theme_minimal()

print(p_seq_viz)
```
```{r}
# ==========================================
# HIGH-PRECISION MULTI-ID TIMELINE (Rug Plot)
# ==========================================
library(tidyverse)

# 1. Detect time column and select IDs
detect_time <- ifelse("Time" %in% names(raw_data), "Time", names(raw_data)[1])

# Selecting 3 representative IDs to avoid cluttering
sample_ids <- unique(raw_data$ID)[1:3]

# 2. Data Preparation
multi_id_data <- raw_data %>%
  filter(ID %in% sample_ids) %>%
  mutate(Activity = trimws(as.character(Activity))) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Activity))

# 3. Generating the Faceted Rug Plot
p_multi_timeline <- ggplot(multi_id_data, aes(x = get(detect_time), y = 1, fill = Activity)) +
  # geom_tile creates solid blocks of color per second
  geom_tile(width = 1.5) + 
  # Separate horizontal bars for each Participant
  facet_wrap(~ID, ncol = 1, scales = "free_x") +
  # Professional color scheme
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set1", name = "Activity Type") +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = NULL) +
  labs(title = "Experimental Activity Sequence (Sample IDs)",
       subtitle = "Horizontal bars show chronological blocks. White gaps represent sensor transitions or missing labels.",
       x = "Chronological Time Index (Seconds)", y = "Participant") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 10),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    axis.title.y = element_blank()
  )

# ==========================================
# OUTPUT
# ==========================================
print(p_multi_timeline)
```
```{r}
# ==========================================
# FINAL AVAILABILITY MATRIX (Binary 0/1)
# ==========================================
library(tidyverse)

# 1. Identificar sensores reales
sensors <- c("ECG", "Apple.Watch", "Empatica", "Garmin", "Fitbit", "Miband", "Biovotion")
available_sensors <- names(raw_data)[names(raw_data) %in% sensors]

# 2. Limpiar columna de actividades
raw_data <- raw_data %>%
  mutate(Activity = trimws(as.character(Activity))) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Activity))

# 3. Crear matriz de presencia de Actividades
act_presence <- raw_data %>%
  group_by(ID, Skin.Tone, Activity) %>%
  summarise(Present = 1, .groups = "drop") %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = Activity, values_from = Present, values_fill = 0) %>%
  rename_with(~paste0("Act_", .), -c(ID, Skin.Tone))

# 4. Crear matriz de presencia de Sensores
dev_presence <- raw_data %>%
  group_by(ID, Skin.Tone) %>%
  summarise(across(all_of(available_sensors), ~ as.numeric(any(!is.na(.)))), .groups = "drop") %>%
  rename_with(~paste0("Dev_", .), -c(ID, Skin.Tone))

# 5. Unir ambas y preparar para el gráfico
final_matrix <- full_join(act_presence, dev_presence, by = c("ID", "Skin.Tone"))

plot_data <- final_matrix %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = starts_with(c("Act_", "Dev_")), 
               names_to = "Feature", 
               values_to = "Status") %>%
  mutate(
    Feature = str_replace_all(Feature, "Act_|Dev_", ""),
    # ID formateado con el Tono de Piel para el eje Y
    ID_Label = factor(paste0("ID: ", ID, " (ST: ", Skin.Tone, ")"))
  )

# 6. Gráfico Final: Azul (1) o Gris (0)
p_final_binary <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = Feature, y = reorder(ID_Label, Skin.Tone), fill = as.factor(Status))) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.1) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("0" = "#f2f2f2", "1" = "#2c3e50"), 
                    labels = c("Absent", "Present"), name = "Status") +
  labs(title = "Subject Participation & Device Availability Matrix",
       subtitle = "Rows sorted by Skin Tone (Fitzpatrick Scale). Dark blocks indicate data presence.",
       x = "Activities and Sensors", y = "Participant") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 4),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    legend.position = "bottom"
  )

# ==========================================
# EXECUTION
# ==========================================
print(p_final_binary)

# Resumen rápido para tu reporte
cat("\n--- PARTICIPATION SUMMARY (Top 5 rows) ---\n")
print(head(final_matrix, 5))
```

## MODELOO

```{r}
# ==========================================
# MEASUREMENT AGREEMENT: ERROR CALCULATION
# ==========================================

# Calculamos el error absoluto para cada sensor respecto al ECG
error_data <- raw_data %>%
  filter(!is.na(ECG)) %>%
  # Creamos columnas de error (Reloj - ECG)
  mutate(across(all_of(setdiff(available_sensors, "ECG")), 
                ~ abs(. - ECG), 
                .names = "Err_{.col}")) %>%
  # Seleccionamos solo lo importante
  select(ID, Skin.Tone, Activity, starts_with("Err_"))

# Resumen de MAE (Mean Absolute Error) por Tono de Piel
mae_summary <- error_data %>%
  group_by(Skin.Tone) %>%
  summarise(across(starts_with("Err_"), ~ mean(., na.rm = TRUE)))

print("--- MEAN ABSOLUTE ERROR (MAE) BY SKIN TONE ---")
print(mae_summary)
```

```{r}
save.image(file = "datoslimpios.RData")
```
